description_long: This is a large, military-style airplane with a dark green camouflage paint scheme and white cross insignias on the wings and fuselage, reminiscent of World War II German aircraft. The airplane features a high-mounted, straight wing with four engines mounted along the leading edge, each with a visible propeller. The fuselage is robust and cylindrical, with a rounded nose and a cockpit positioned at the front, featuring multiple windows. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer, forming a conventional tail design. The undercarriage is not clearly visible, but the overall structure suggests a heavy transport or bomber role. The surface appears matte and hard, with minimal gloss and a utilitarian finish.
description: A large, dark green military airplane with four propeller engines, high straight wings, and a conventional tail, featuring white cross insignias and a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, cockpit windows, and the four engines with propellers along the high-mounted wing.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight, high-mounted wingspan, engine placement, and the cross insignias on the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the tail, engines, and fuselage details from the other side.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the underside of the wings, engine nacelles, and the tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the leading edge of the wing, each with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not clearly visible; likely retractable based on typical design, but not confirmed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
